programmes.show_description: Exploring The Purple One's musical DNA in the rave with an hour of Prince-inspired electronic music.
6 Music takes you to the dark side with the finest goth, electronic and dark pop music, featuring The Cramps, Kathryn Joseph, Gesaffelstein, Whipping Boy, aya and more.
The jazz keyboardist and producer selects the first hour of this focus mix. Featuring tracks from Slum Village, Hiatus Kaiyote, Dilla, Madlib and more.
Inspired by summer by the sea, Christina Vantzou selects two hours of ambient tracks to deepen your concentration, including music by Holland Andrews, Gail Loughton and Coby Sey .
Need to slow things down? Afrodeutsche brings the mellow Saturday flow you need to really hit that deeply relaxing vibe.
Mark and Stuart present a feast of great music and actor James McAvoy shares his 'First, Last and Everything' and chats about his latest film and directorial debut.
Beth's back for round two with musical gems new and old, plus a Caffeine Kick at 11!
Zakia sits in for Jamz and chats to Ego Ella May about her new album Good Intentions. They talk about the making of the record, themes running through it and more.
Gilles invites Ezra Collective's drummer, Femi Koleoso, and saxophonist Courtney Pine into the studio to celebrate two milestones in British jazz.

programmes.show_name: The Stuart Maconie Funk and Soul Show
programmes.show_description: Andy Platts from Mamas Gun is Stuart's guest, talking about the new album DIG!.
Don breaks format with an essential selection of reggae covers. Expect bass-heavy takes on the likes of The Beatles, Bronski Beat, Ray Charles, Dusty Springfield and Blur.
SHERELLE lights up your Saturday night with some high-NRG drum and bass.
Stalwart of the drum and bass scene, Breakage, takes over the Sweatbox with 30 minutes of his own material.
Celebrating the eclectic remix catalogue of Kieran Hebden, better known as Four Tet.
Sit back and enjoy Divorce's very own curated Morning After Mix featuring Labi Siffre, Joni Mitchell and Mitski.
A hand-crafted, two-hour mix of chilled music old and new. With tracks from Frank Ocean, Scott Walker, Mitski, Little Dragon, The National and more.
Let Afrodeutsche be your guide into a sublime state of relaxation. Her musical flow is exactly what you need to start your Sunday morning just right.
Mark and Stuart present a feast of great music and chat for weekend breakfast, plus they celebrate the 50th anniversary of pioneering punk band the Ramones' debut album.
Cerys is joined by Tauseef Akhtar in session and film scholar Rachel Dwyer, exploring Bollywood's poetic traditions with live ghazals, rich cinema insight and archive moments.
Marc Riley is in for Guy, bringing you his Finest Flour (and no, that's not a misprint). He's even got a game you can play at home.
Jarvis takes you on a sonic trip through time and space, the Peak District and a village that may or may not exist.
With a borderless spectrum of music, new experiments in storytelling and sound.
Stuart's featured album is the self titled debut by German cult legends Faust. Plus, experimental jazz from Belgium and classic UK Psyche. Let's go!
Zakia pulls out some old favourites inspired by the recent burst of sunshine and is joined by south London producer Kwes to share the inspiration behind his second album, Kinds.
